changed_functions.name: AfdSanFastCompleteRequest (0x1C0077700 / 0x1C00787A0)
key_changes: NEW state check added at patched 0x1c0078afb: 'cmp byte [r15+0x91], 0x0' — refuses to queue the IRP onto an endpoint whose abort byte is set; the IRP is instead completed with STATUS_CANCELLED (0xc0000120)

The new check is gated by a Windows feature-staging flag: EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails) at patched 0x1c0078af2. When the feature is disabled the code falls through (0x1c0078af9 je 0x1c0078b05) to the identical unpatched queuing path (old path retained in else branch)
The IRP-list insertion at [endpoint+0x70] is performed under the endpoint spinlock (KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock on [endpoint+0x30]) in BOTH builds; the added check runs under that same lock
AfdSanCancelRequest is the same cancel routine in both builds (relocated 0x1c0076090 -> 0x1c0077190)
The AccessMode && Length!=0 guard before the buffer poke is present identically in both builds (decompiler register renaming only; not a behavior change)
changed_functions.security_notes: AfdSanFastCompleteRequest is an AFD SAN (Sockets-Direct) completion handler. In both builds the IRP is linked into the endpoint pending list at [endpoint+0x70] while the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30] is held, so the insertion is already serialized. The patch adds, under that same lock and behind a feature-staging flag, a refusal to queue when the endpoint abort byte [endpoint+0x91] is set (completing the IRP STATUS_CANCELLED instead). This is a defense-in-depth hardening state check, staged behind a feature flag with the prior behavior retained when the flag is off. It is not a demonstrable, reachable use-after-free: no lock is removed, no lifetime/refcount change is made, and no freed-object dereference or exploit primitive is present in or reachable from this function on the evidence in the two builds.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1c0077700) — IRP queuing path under the endpoint spinlock:
  0x1c0077a15: lea  rcx, [r15+0x30]                 ; endpoint spinlock
  0x1c0077a21: call KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock
  0x1c0077a27: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c0077a2e: xchg [rdi+0x68], rax                 ; set IRP->CancelRoutine
  0x1c0077a32: cmp  byte [rdi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0077a36: jne  0x1c0077a75                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0077a38: add  rdi, 0xa8
  0x1c0077a3f: lea  rax, [r15+0x70]                 ; endpoint IRP list head
  0x1c0077a53: mov  [rdi], rcx                      ; InsertTailList (under lock)
  0x1c0077a69: call KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

PATCHED (0x1c00787a0) — same lock, added feature-gated abort-state check:
  0x1c0078ac8: lea  rcx, [r15+0x30]                 ; endpoint spinlock
  0x1c0078ad4: call KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock
  0x1c0078ada: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c0078ae1: xchg [rsi+0x68], rax
  0x1c0078ae5: cmp  byte [rsi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0078ae9: jne  0x1c0078b42                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0078aeb: lea  rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1c0078af2: call EvaluateCurrentState            ; feature-staging gate
  0x1c0078af7: test eax, eax
  0x1c0078af9: je   0x1c0078b05                     ; feature OFF -> old queuing path
  0x1c0078afb: cmp  byte [r15+0x91], 0x0            ; endpoint abort state
  0x1c0078b03: jne  0x1c0078b42                     ; if aborting -> STATUS_CANCELLED
  0x1c0078b05: add  rsi, 0xa8                       ; queue (under lock)
  0x1c0078b36: call KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

changed_functions.name: AfdSanRedirectRequest (0x1C00785E4 / 0x1C0079734)
key_changes: NEW state check added at patched 0x1c0079819: 'cmp byte [rsi+0x91], 0x0' — same endpoint abort-state gate as AfdSanFastCompleteRequest, completing the IRP STATUS_CANCELLED instead of queuing when set
Gated by EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails) at patched 0x1c0079810; when disabled (0x1c0079817 je 0x1c0079826) the original queuing path runs unchanged
The IRP-list insertion at [endpoint+0x70] runs under the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30] in BOTH builds; AfdSanCancelRequest is the same cancel routine (relocated 0x1c0076090 -> 0x1c0077190)
changed_functions.security_notes: AfdSanRedirectRequest is an AFD SAN (Sockets-Direct) redirect-request handler, invoked from AfdDispatch/AfdReceive/AfdSend only for SAN/switch endpoints. Same shape as AfdSanFastCompleteRequest: the whole handler runs under the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30], and the patch adds under that lock a feature-staged refusal to queue onto an endpoint whose abort byte [endpoint+0x91] is set. Defense-in-depth hardening state check, staged behind a feature flag with the old path retained. Not a demonstrable, reachable use-after-free.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1c00785e4) — IRP linked into list under the endpoint spinlock, no abort-byte check:
  0x1c00786a4: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c00786ab: xchg [rbx+0x68], rax                 ; set IRP->CancelRoutine
  0x1c00786af: cmp  byte [rbx+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c00786b3: je   0x1c00786f2                     ; not cancelled -> queue
  0x1c00786f2: ...  link IRP into [rsi+0x70] under spinlock, then release

PATCHED (0x1c0079734) — adds feature-gated abort-byte check before queuing:
  0x1c00797f4: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c00797fb: xchg [rdi+0x68], rax
  0x1c00797ff: cmp  byte [rdi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0079803: jne  0x1c00798aa                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0079809: lea  rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1c0079810: call EvaluateCurrentState            ; feature-staging gate
  0x1c0079815: test eax, eax
  0x1c0079817: je   0x1c0079826                     ; feature OFF -> old queuing path
  0x1c0079819: cmp  byte [rsi+0x91], 0x0            ; endpoint abort state
  0x1c0079820: jne  0x1c00798aa                     ; if aborting -> STATUS_CANCELLED
  0x1c0079826: ...  link IRP into [rsi+0x70] under spinlock, then release

The sub_1c0047f50 function (now sub_1c0015af8) creates transport connection objects via IoCreateFile with proper handle validation.

security_findings.entry_point: AFD SAN (Winsock Sockets-Direct) request path; the sibling AfdSanRedirectRequest is reached from AfdDispatch/AfdReceive/AfdSend for SAN/switch endpoints only.
call_chain: AFD SAN (Sockets-Direct) request dispatch on a SAN/switch endpoint
Endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30] acquired; IRP linked into endpoint pending list at [endpoint+0x70] under that lock in BOTH builds
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Defense-in-depth state check under existing lock (hardening; not a demonstrated CWE-416/CWE-362)
security_findings.description: In both builds AfdSanFastCompleteRequest links the IRP into the endpoint pending list at [endpoint+0x70] while holding the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30], so the insertion is already serialized. The patch adds, under that same lock and after the IRP->Cancel check, a call to EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails) (a Windows feature-staging flag) followed by 'cmp byte [endpoint+0x91], 0'. When the feature is enabled and the endpoint abort byte is set, the IRP is completed with STATUS_CANCELLED (0xc0000120) instead of being queued; when the feature is disabled the code falls through to the identical unpatched queuing path (old path retained). This is a feature-staged hardening state check, not a demonstrable use-after-free: no lock is removed, no lifetime/refcount change is made, and no reachable freed-object dereference or exploit primitive is present in the two builds.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No exploitable condition is demonstrable from the two builds. The changed code is the AFD SAN (Sockets-Direct) path, reached only for SAN/switch endpoints. Both builds insert the IRP under the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30]; the patch only adds, under that lock and behind a feature-staging flag, a refusal to queue onto an endpoint whose abort byte [endpoint+0x91] is already set.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The added instructions are a state check performed while the endpoint spinlock is held; they do not create or expose a memory-corruption primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED (0x1c0077700) — IRP queuing path under the endpoint spinlock:
  0x1c0077a15: lea  rcx, [r15+0x30]                 ; endpoint spinlock
  0x1c0077a21: call KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock
  0x1c0077a27: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c0077a2e: xchg [rdi+0x68], rax                 ; set IRP->CancelRoutine
  0x1c0077a32: cmp  byte [rdi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0077a36: jne  0x1c0077a75                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0077a38: add  rdi, 0xa8
  0x1c0077a3f: lea  rax, [r15+0x70]                 ; endpoint IRP list head
  0x1c0077a53: mov  [rdi], rcx                      ; InsertTailList (under lock)
  0x1c0077a69: call KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

PATCHED (0x1c00787a0) — same lock, added feature-gated abort-state check:
  0x1c0078ac8: lea  rcx, [r15+0x30]                 ; endpoint spinlock
  0x1c0078ad4: call KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock
  0x1c0078ada: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c0078ae1: xchg [rsi+0x68], rax
  0x1c0078ae5: cmp  byte [rsi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0078ae9: jne  0x1c0078b42                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0078aeb: lea  rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1c0078af2: call EvaluateCurrentState            ; feature-staging gate
  0x1c0078af7: test eax, eax
  0x1c0078af9: je   0x1c0078b05                     ; feature OFF -> old queuing path
  0x1c0078afb: cmp  byte [r15+0x91], 0x0            ; endpoint abort state
  0x1c0078b03: jne  0x1c0078b42                     ; if aborting -> STATUS_CANCELLED
  0x1c0078b05: add  rsi, 0xa8                       ; queue (under lock)
  0x1c0078b36: call KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

breakpoints.why: Break at the SAN completion handler entry to observe the changed queuing path. In the patched build, single-step to the EvaluateCurrentState call and the following 'cmp byte [endpoint+0x91], 0'.

Break at the IRP->Cancel check. r15 = endpoint (r15+0x30 = spinlock held here, r15+0x70 = IRP list head, r15+0x91 = abort byte); rsi = IRP (rsi+0x44 = IRP->Cancel, rsi+0x68 = IRP->CancelRoutine). The added feature-gated 0x91 check follows.

watch: r15 (patched) / r15 (unpatched) = AFD SAN endpoint. r15+0x30 = spinlock (held across the check and the insertion), r15+0x70 = IRP list head, r15+0x91 = abort state byte, r15+0x8c = connection state (0x103 = STATUS_PENDING).
IRP pointer: rsi (patched) / rdi (unpatched). +0x44 = IRP->Cancel, +0x68 = IRP->CancelRoutine, +0xa8 = list entry being inserted.
key_offsets: 0x1c0077a15/0x1c0078ac8: KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock on [endpoint+0x30] — the insertion is lock-protected in both builds
0x1c0078afb: cmp byte [r15+0x91], 0x0 — endpoint abort-state check added in the patch
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable: no vulnerability to reproduce. For inspection only, break at the two handler addresses and compare the queuing path with and without the feature gate.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No crash is expected from the change itself. In the patched build with the feature enabled and the endpoint abort byte set, the handler completes the IRP with STATUS_CANCELLED (0xc0000120) instead of queuing it.
debugging_aids.notes: The gate is a Windows feature-staging (velocity) flag evaluated by EvaluateCurrentState; the descriptor is g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails. When the flag is disabled the code path is identical to the unpatched build (old path retained in the else branch).

security_findings.entry_point: NtDeviceIoControlFile on an AFD socket bound to a SAN/switch endpoint; AfdSanRedirectRequest is called from AfdDispatch, AfdReceive, and AfdSend.
call_chain: NtDeviceIoControlFile on a SAN/switch AFD endpoint
Whole handler runs under the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30]; IRP linked into [endpoint+0x70] under that lock in BOTH builds
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Defense-in-depth state check under existing lock (hardening; not a demonstrated CWE-416/CWE-362)
security_findings.description: AfdSanRedirectRequest is an AFD SAN (Sockets-Direct) redirect-request handler, not an event/select or poll handler. In both builds the handler runs entirely under the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30], validates the endpoint (magic 0x1AFD at [endpoint+0], type byte 4 at [endpoint+2], connection state at [endpoint+0x8c]), sets AfdSanCancelRequest, checks IRP->Cancel, and links the IRP into the endpoint list at [endpoint+0x70]. The patch adds, under the same lock and after the IRP->Cancel check, EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails) followed by 'cmp byte [endpoint+0x91], 0'. When the feature is enabled and the endpoint is aborting, the IRP is completed STATUS_CANCELLED instead of being queued; otherwise the original path runs. Feature-staged hardening state check, old path retained; not a demonstrable use-after-free.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No exploitable condition is demonstrable from the two builds. Reachability is limited to the SAN/switch endpoint path. Both builds link the IRP under the endpoint spinlock; the patch adds a feature-gated refusal to queue onto an endpoint whose abort byte [endpoint+0x91] is set.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The added check runs while the endpoint spinlock is held and does not create or expose a memory-corruption primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED (0x1c00785e4) — IRP linked into list under the endpoint spinlock, no abort-byte check:
  0x1c00786a4: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c00786ab: xchg [rbx+0x68], rax                 ; set IRP->CancelRoutine
  0x1c00786af: cmp  byte [rbx+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c00786b3: je   0x1c00786f2                     ; not cancelled -> queue
  0x1c00786f2: ...  link IRP into [rsi+0x70] under spinlock, then release

PATCHED (0x1c0079734) — adds feature-gated abort-byte check before queuing:
  0x1c00797f4: lea  rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
  0x1c00797fb: xchg [rdi+0x68], rax
  0x1c00797ff: cmp  byte [rdi+0x44], 0x0            ; IRP->Cancel
  0x1c0079803: jne  0x1c00798aa                     ; if cancelled -> cancel path
  0x1c0079809: lea  rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1c0079810: call EvaluateCurrentState            ; feature-staging gate
  0x1c0079815: test eax, eax
  0x1c0079817: je   0x1c0079826                     ; feature OFF -> old queuing path
  0x1c0079819: cmp  byte [rsi+0x91], 0x0            ; endpoint abort state
  0x1c0079820: jne  0x1c00798aa                     ; if aborting -> STATUS_CANCELLED
  0x1c0079826: ...  link IRP into [rsi+0x70] under spinlock, then release

breakpoints.why: Break at the SAN redirect handler entry. rcx=IRP, rdx=arg2; the endpoint is loaded as rsi = *( *(rdx+0x30) + 0x18 ), and the endpoint spinlock [rsi+0x30] is acquired before the state validation and IRP insertion.
Break at the EvaluateCurrentState call to observe the added feature-staged 'cmp byte [rsi+0x91], 0' check. rsi = endpoint, rdi = IRP.

watch: rsi = AFD SAN endpoint. rsi+0x30 = spinlock (held), rsi+0x70 = IRP list head, rsi+0x91 = abort state byte, rsi+0x8c = connection state (0x103 = pending), rsi+0 = magic 0x1AFD.
IRP pointer: rbx (unpatched) / rdi (patched). +0x44 = IRP->Cancel, +0x68 = IRP->CancelRoutine.
key_offsets: 0x1c007860e/0x1c007975e: KeAcquireInStackQueuedSpinLock on [endpoint+0x30] — insertion is lock-protected in both builds
0x1c0079819: cmp byte [rsi+0x91], 0x0 — endpoint abort-state check added in the patch
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable: no vulnerability to reproduce. For inspection only, break at the two handler addresses on a SAN/switch endpoint and compare the queuing path with and without the feature gate.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No crash is expected from the change itself. With the feature enabled and the endpoint abort byte set, the patched handler completes the IRP STATUS_CANCELLED (0xc0000120) instead of queuing it.
debugging_aids.notes: Same feature-staging gate as AfdSanFastCompleteRequest: EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails). AfdSanCancelRequest is the same cancel routine in both builds (relocated 0x1c0076090 -> 0x1c0077190).

summary: The patch adds a feature-staged hardening state check inside two AFD SAN (Winsock Sockets-Direct) request handlers: AfdSanFastCompleteRequest (0x1c0077700 / 0x1c00787a0) and AfdSanRedirectRequest (0x1c00785e4 / 0x1c0079734). In both builds these handlers link an IRP into the endpoint pending list at [endpoint+0x70] while holding the endpoint spinlock [endpoint+0x30], so the insertion is already serialized. The patch adds, under that same lock and behind a Windows feature-staging flag (EvaluateCurrentState on g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails), a refusal to queue when the endpoint abort byte [endpoint+0x91] is set, completing the IRP with STATUS_CANCELLED (0xc0000120) instead. When the flag is disabled the code falls through to the identical unpatched queuing path (old path retained in the else branch). This is a defense-in-depth hardening check on the niche Sockets-Direct path, not a demonstrable, reachable use-after-free: no lock is removed, no lifetime/refcount change is made, and no freed-object dereference or exploit primitive is present in or reachable from these functions on the evidence in the two builds. Rated informational.
